use_1g_hugepages.description:
Server-authoritative decision on whether to back this VM's memory
with 1 GiB hugepages.  When true, the host MUST verify a 1 GiB
hugetlbfs mount exists and emit hugepage XML.  When false, the host
MUST NOT emit hugepage backing even if its sysfs reports hugepages —
the server has determined the host's hugepage budget is unknown
or zero (most often during rolling upgrades when the DB hasn't
caught up to the host's current state).  `#[serde(default)]` keeps
older payloads backwards-compatible: a missing field deserializes
to `false`, which is the safe direction.

gpu_to_cell.description:
(passthrough_position, guest_cell_id) for each passthrough GPU whose
host NUMA node corresponds to one of the guest cells.  The first u32
is the GPU's POSITION in the passthrough list (i.e. its index in the
host's `vgpus` Vec, which mirrors the order of `gpu_indices` requested
by the server), not the hardware GPU index.  Position semantics let
the XML emitter look the cell up directly from its enumerate loop
without plumbing hardware indices through `VmGpuDeviceConfig`.

Lets the host emit each GPU hostdev behind the pxb-pcie bus of the
matching guest cell so `nvidia-smi topo -m` and
`/sys/bus/pci/.../numa_node` inside the guest report real per-GPU
NUMA affinity.  Empty when the host has no per-GPU NUMA data or when
no GPUs are passed through.  May be a strict subset of the
passthrough positions (e.g. one GPU has `numa_node = None`); the
host falls back to the legacy flat layout when the mapping is
incomplete.

`#[serde(default)]` keeps wire compatibility with older desktops
that pre-date Plan B (rolling upgrades): they ignore the field on
receive, and a new desktop reading from an old server gets an
empty Vec → falls back to the existing flat hostdev placement.
